title: Renovations
metaTitle: Joinery-Led Renovations Sydney | InsideOut Joinery & Renos
metaDescription: Joinery-led renovations across Sydney including kitchens, wardrobes, laundries, vanities and full-home fitouts, with real project examples that show how the rooms work together once the joinery is resolved.
intro: Our renovation work is joinery-led. That means we focus on the cabinetry, storage, room planning and finish coordination that make a renovation feel properly resolved once the dust settles. Sometimes that starts with a kitchen. Other times it is a broader scope where wardrobes, vanities, laundries and living-room joinery all need to feel like part of the same home.
included: Kitchen renovations where the cabinetry sets the tone for the rest of the room
Wardrobes, vanities and laundries that are designed to feel connected rather than piecemeal
Home fitout joinery that carries one material and detailing language from room to room
Renovation planning that considers how the joinery ties into floors, splashbacks, stone and surrounding finishes
Multi-room cabinetry scopes quoted together so the storage, layout and finish decisions stay cohesive
Project-led guidance based on real Sydney homes rather than catalogue assumptions

featureBlocks.title: Kitchen-first renovations
featureBlocks.text: In many homes the kitchen is the room that drives the whole renovation. When the joinery plan is right, it helps the rest of the house feel more coherent too.

featureBlocks.title: Supporting rooms still matter
featureBlocks.text: Wardrobes, laundries, vanities and living joinery are often what make a renovation feel complete. Treating them properly stops the home feeling resolved in one room and unfinished everywhere else.

featureBlocks.title: One joinery language across the home
featureBlocks.text: Projects such as Willoughby, Darlinghurst and Epping show how much stronger the final result feels when the joinery shares a clear point of view from room to room.

materialsTitle: Where joinery-led renovations add the most value
materials: A more consistent finish schedule across kitchens, wardrobes, laundries and vanities
Storage that solves real daily issues instead of being added as an afterthought
Cleaner visual links between renovated rooms and their adjoining spaces
A simpler quote-to-installation pathway for the cabinetry scope inside a larger renovation

beforeAfter.title: Before & after
beforeAfter.text: Willoughby is a good example of a renovation where the kitchen and supporting joinery changed the feel of the whole home rather than just one room.
beforeAfter.beforeAlt: Willoughby renovation before new kitchen and supporting joinery
beforeAfter.afterAlt: Willoughby renovation after kitchen and supporting joinery fitout

faqs.question: Do you only renovate kitchens?
faqs.answer: No. Kitchens are a major part of what we do, but we also handle wardrobes, laundries, vanities and wider joinery fitouts as part of renovation work.

faqs.question: Is your renovation work still joinery-focused?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our strongest value is in the joinery scope — cabinetry, storage planning, finish coordination and the details that tie rooms together.

faqs.question: Can multiple rooms be quoted together?
faqs.answer: Yes. In many homes it makes sense to quote the kitchen, wardrobes, vanities and laundry together so the project can be planned more cohesively.

faqs.question: Can you work from plans and inspiration images?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Plans, site photos and reference images are all helpful for shaping the renovation scope and giving clearer quote guidance.

ctaText: We can help map out the joinery so the finished rooms feel connected, practical and worth the investment instead of pieced together room by room.
